scope: full-mesh R3 re-sweep (43 specialists over every cataloged repo) after the Movement I heal, adjudicated finding-by-finding
gate: zero unexplained drift — every finding is fixed or carries an active waiver
legs: IN-SYNC — ecosystem-spec byte-identical across all three homes; agent action enum is a strict superset of required_actions
conformance: kody-w/rapp-map/conformance — golden drift cases (8 classes + 3 historical), zero-dep runner, and the waiver ledger that explains the 4 waived findings; any future sweep validates itself against it before its verdict is trusted
note: Detection layer of the ecosystem immune system: the sweep DETECTS, these frames REMEMBER. Public-by-construction (the body's DOG rule): no customer names, no private data. The R2-era record this file replaces (DRIFT-FOUND, 13 findings) is preserved in the biography — frame history is the memory, not this pointer.

findings_corroborated.detail: A neighborhood front door minted identities in a format that was never canon; the mint path now emits the Eternity form under the identity law (sha256 of the exported master public key), and stored legacy identities canonicalize on read with their keys preserved. Merged upstream.
A hub README presented the legacy bare-slug identity shape as canonical; it now shows the owner-scoped Eternity form with the legacy shape annotated read-forever. Merged upstream.
One mesh neuron still watched for a retired identity model and flagged a conforming repo; the detector, not the repo, was corrected — re-baselined as a traceable commit.

corroboration_note: The 4 waived findings (policy-only constitution articles vs. an unchanged schema string; pre-ratification presentation lag; a lossless read-forever migration; a frozen planted bundle awaiting its refresh ritual) are ledgered with canon citations in the conformance waiver ledger.
